1,Ethical,Algorithmic Bias in Credit Scoring - Training data may reflect historical discrimination leading to digital redlining (e.g. minority applicants unfairly denied mortgage loans). Biased outcomes could disproportionately impact protected classes in loan approvals.,H,VH,Critical,"Implement fairness-aware machine learning techniques and regular bias audits to identify and reduce discriminatory patterns in model outcomes. Retrain the model using diverse and representative datasets to minimize digital redlining risks.","1. Conduct quarterly fairness audits using demographic parity analysis - METRIC: Demographic parity ratio ≥0.80 across all protected classes (race, gender, age)
2. Apply bias-mitigation algorithms (e.g., reweighting, adversarial debiasing) and ensure training dataset diversity - METRIC: Training data includes ≥30% minority applicants
3. Review findings with AI Ethics Council - METRIC: Fairness gap between majority/minority approval rates <5%
4. Track audit completion and remediation - METRIC: Quarterly audit completion rate 100%
column2: Critical findings resolved within 45 days",Data Science Team Lead,"Initial fairness audit due Q1 2025. Current baseline: demographic parity ratio 0.72 (below threshold). Action: Retrain model with enhanced minority representation. Target: ≥0.80 by Q2 2025."

column1: 2,Legal,Violation of Fair Lending Laws (ECOA FHA) - Automated decisions could result in discriminatory outcomes violating Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA) and Fair Housing Act (FHA). Legal liability and regulatory penalties.,H,VH,Critical,"Integrate human oversight for high-impact lending decisions and perform annual compliance reviews aligned with ECOA and FHA. Engage legal team to ensure all AI decisions meet federal lending standards and CFPB guidance.","1. Integrate human-in-the-loop review for all high-risk loan decisions (>$500K or applicants flagged by fairness algorithm) - METRIC: Human review rate for flagged decisions >95%
2. Legal and compliance teams conduct annual comprehensive audit against ECOA/FHA requirements - METRIC: Zero ECOA/FHA violations annually
3. Track regulatory complaints and legal actions - METRIC: Consumer complaints related to discrimination = 0

4. Maintain documentation for adverse action notices - METRIC: Adverse action notice compliance 100%",Compliance Officer,"Awaiting legal policy update per CFPB guidance on AI in lending. Last ECOA audit: Oct 2024 - no critical findings. Next full compliance review: Annual (Jan 2025)."

3,Data Privacy / Security,Loss or Unauthorized Disclosure of Applicant Data - Sensitive personal and financial data (SSN credit history income bank statements) could be exposed through API vulnerabilities cloud misconfiguration or insider threats.  GLBA and state privacy law violations.,H,VH,Critical,"Apply FIPS 140-2 validated encryption for all PII and financial data at rest and in transit. Enforce strict identity and access management (IAM) with zero-trust architecture. Include data protection clauses in all vendor contracts. Conduct regular penetration testing aligned with NIST SP 800-53 security controls.","1. Implement FIPS 140-2 validated encryption for all applicant data - METRIC: Encryption coverage for PII/financial data 100%
2. Enforce vendor NDAs and zero-trust network access controls - METRIC: Vendor security compliance audits 100%
3. Conduct annual penetration testing and vulnerability assessments - METRIC: Penetration test pass rate >95% (critical/high vulnerabilities remediated)
4. Monitor data access and detect anomalies - METRIC: Data breach incidents = 0
column2: Unauthorized access attempts detected and blocked >99%

column1: 5. Employee security training - METRIC: Annual security training completion 100%
column2: Phishing simulation pass rate >90%",Cybersecurity Lead,"Last penetration test: Oct 2024 - no critical findings. 2 medium vulnerabilities patched within SLA. Next test: Oct 2025. Current encryption coverage: 100% for production data."

column1: 4,Technical / Operational,Model Drift or Performance Degradation - Changes in economic conditions applicant behavior or market dynamics reduce model accuracy over time. Increased false positives/negatives leading to revenue loss or increased defaults.,H,VH,Critical,"Continuously monitor model performance metrics including accuracy precision recall and AUC-ROC. Retrain the algorithm quarterly or when key performance indicators fall below acceptable thresholds. Implement automated alerts for performance degradation.","1. Implement real-time model performance tracking dashboard - METRIC: Model AUC-ROC maintained >0.85
2. Monitor recall rate for loan defaults - METRIC: Recall (sensitivity) for identifying high-risk applicants >85%
3. Quarterly model retraining using rolling 24-month dataset - METRIC: Quarterly retraining completion 100%
4. Automated performance alerts - METRIC: Alert triggered if accuracy drops >3% week-over-week
5. A/B testing for model updates - METRIC: New model versions tested on ≥10% sample before full deployment
column2: Champion/challenger win rate tracked",ML Engineer / Data Science,"Retraining schedule approved. Current model performance: AUC 0.87 (above threshold). Last retrain: Oct 2024. Monitoring shows stable performance. Next scheduled retrain: Jan 2025."

column1: 5,Transparency / Explainability,Opaque Black Box Model Decisions - Lack of interpretability prevents justification of loan denials violating explainability requirements under ECOA (adverse action notices) and internal governance policies. Applicants cannot understand why they were denied.,H,H,High,"Deploy explainable AI (XAI) tools like SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) or LIME (Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations) to generate understandable decision reasons for each loan recommendation. Provide transparent model documentation and user-friendly explanation summaries for applicants.","1. Adopt and integrate explainable AI framework (SHAP/LIME) into production pipeline - METRIC: XAI reason code generation for all decisions 100%
column2: Model interpretability score (mean absolute SHAP value) >0.7

column1: 2. Generate adverse action reason codes compliant with Reg B (ECOA) - METRIC: Reason code generation for all denials 100%
3. Provide applicant-facing transparency reports explaining key factors - METRIC: Transparency report delivery rate 100%
4. Track and reduce explainability complaints - METRIC: Applicant complaints about unexplained decisions <1% monthly

5. Document model logic and feature importance - METRIC: Model documentation updated within 30 days of each retrain 100%",Data Science & Compliance,"Pilot explainability module (SHAP) under review. Initial testing shows mean SHAP score 0.73. Legal review of reason code templates in progress. Target production deployment: Q2 2025."

column1: 9,Third-Party / Vendor,Third-Party Vendor Dependency and Supply Chain Risk - Heavy reliance on external credit bureaus (Experian TransUnion Equifax) income verification services (The Work Number) and cloud infrastructure providers creates single points of failure.
